summary: Split the tail of melee/gm/gm_1601.c into two new nonmatching game-mode translation units, gm_19EF.c and gmpause.c, with matching split ranges, placeholder source stubs, dedicated headers, and symbol metadata updates. The PR moved prototypes for functions from 0x8019F9C4 through 0x801A1134 out of gm_1601.h into gm_19EF.h and gmpause.h, and annotated many adjacent data/sdata labels as strings in symbols.txt.
what_changed: Shrank the gm_1601.c split ranges so its .text ends at 0x8019EFC4 instead of 0x801A12C4, with corresponding .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 end-address adjustments.

Added a new melee/gm/gm_19EF.c split covering .text 0x8019EFC4-0x801A0E34, .data 0x803DA3E0-0x803DA4B0, .bss 0x80479A98-0x80479B10, .sdata 0x804D41F0-0x804D4218, .sbss 0x804D6698-0x804D6700, and .sdata2 0x804DA950-0x804DA980.

Added a new melee/gm/gmpause.c split covering .text 0x801A0E34-0x801A12C4, .data 0x803DA4B0-0x803DA4F0, .bss 0x80479B10-0x80479B28, .sdata 0x804D4218-0x804D4230, .sbss 0x804D6700-0x804D6708, and .sdata2 0x804DA980-0x804DA998.
Created gm_19EF.c with inline decomp placeholder markers for fn_8019F9C4, gm_801A0A10, gm_801A0B18, fn_801A0B60, gm_801A0C6C, and gm_801A0E0C.
Created gmpause.c with inline decomp placeholder markers for fn_801A0E34, gm_801A0FEC, gm_801A10FC, and fn_801A1134.
Created gm_19EF.h and gmpause.h using placeholder.h and moved the relevant UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes out of gm_1601.h.
Updated configure.py to register melee/gm/gmpause.c as a NonMatching object; the diff excerpt also shows melee/gm/gmintro.c added as NonMatching.

Refined symbols.txt around 0x803DA3E0-0x803DA4D8 and 0x804D4198-0x804D4220 by splitting former aggregate/unknown labels into smaller labels and marking many as data:string, with some local-scope annotations.
smart_moves: Kept the new files nonmatching and used placeholder function markers, allowing the repository layout and split boundaries to improve before individual functions are fully decompiled.
Moved only the prototypes that belong to the newly split address ranges, reducing gm_1601.h scope and making future work easier to localize.
Updated all affected split sections, not just .text, which is important for correct object reconstruction when data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 also move with the split.
Broke a broad .data label at 0x803DA3E0 into multiple string-sized symbols, giving future decompilers better evidence for string references and local data layout.
Separated the likely pause-related functions into gmpause.c while keeping the preceding 0x8019EFC4-0x801A0E34 block in a neutral gm_19EF.c file rather than over-naming uncertain behavior.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large game-mode source file, update config/GALE01/splits.txt section boundaries for every affected segment, not only the text range.
Use small dedicated headers for newly split modules and remove their declarations from the parent monolithic header to avoid misleading ownership.
Placeholder source files with `/// #symbol` markers are a useful intermediate step for carving translation units before full matching work begins.
String metadata in symbols.txt can be improved during a split; marking `data:string` and correct sizes helps future matching and data reconstruction.
If behavior is uncertain, a range-style name such as gm_19EF can be safer than inventing a semantic module name; the PR only comments that it seems to handle "Go!" animations based on .dat filenames.
naming_conventions: New module `gmpause` follows the existing game-mode prefix style for semantically identified gm files.
New module `gm_19EF` uses an address-derived name for a game-mode block whose exact purpose is not firmly established.
Unknown functions remain named with existing address-based conventions such as `fn_8019F9C4` and `gm_801A0A10`.
Headers use uppercase include guards matching the path/module, e.g. `MELEE_GM_GM_19EF_H` and `MELEE_GM_GMPAUSE_H`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjusted split boundaries at exact function/data boundaries: gm_1601.c ends at 0x8019EFC4, gm_19EF.c covers 0x8019EFC4-0x801A0E34, and gmpause.c covers 0x801A0E34-0x801A12C4.
Registered newly split modules as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` in configure.py so they can exist in the build while still relying on extracted/nonmatching code.
Used `/// #function` markers to bind decompilation stubs to known symbols without attempting C implementations in this PR.
Refined symbol sizes and string classifications to support correct data references for future matching attempts.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile functions in src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.c, especially fn_8019F9C4 or gm_801A0A10.
Find later PRs that decompile or rename gmpause functions fn_801A0E34, gm_801A0FEC, gm_801A10FC, or fn_801A1134.
Check whether the comment about gm_19EF handling "Go!" animations was later confirmed or replaced with a semantic module name.
Search for later symbol refinements around 0x803DA3E0-0x803DA4F0 and 0x804D41F0-0x804D4230.
